mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following low FEDFUNDS observations do not exceed zero in a statistically significant proportion of out-of-sample months (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in nonfarm payrolls indicates contracting labor income and weakening aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months where PAYEMS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and rising industrial production indicates demand-pull expansion that outpaces supply constraints, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; neither series alone captures the joint momentum.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and INDPRO yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in predicting 60-day SPX returns in a rolling 3-year regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near zero, accelerations in CPI inflation signal successful reflation and reduce real debt burdens, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days; this direction reverses in high-rate regimes where inflation triggers tightening expectations.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, rising CPIAUCSL does not produce positive average 20-day SPX excess returns (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production relative to its trailing 12-month level indicates negative operating leverage and margin compression for goods-producing firms, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days once the year-over-year growth rate turns negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters when INDPRO year-over-year growth is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A surprise decline in the unemployment rate within a single month signals tightening labor markets and imminent wage-driven inflation, prompting equity market fears of near-term monetary tightening and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 5-day returns in fewer than 50% of months where UNRATE declines month-over-month during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and payroll employment confirms that consumer spending is supported by income growth rather than credit drawdown, creating a sustainable demand impulse that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS and PAYEMS interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day SPX returns in a pooled regression.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP is below its prior-year level, a concurrent rise in retail sales signals inventory restocking and demand normalization, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; this signal is absent when GDP is already expanding.
failure_condition: In quarters where GDPC1 year-over-year growth is negative, positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not produce positive average 20-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05).
